statement.en: Development and optimisation of a novel microcapillary and smartphone based global haemostasis and platelet function assay
statement.summary: Platelets are small cells that play a crucial role in haemostasis. Platelet dysfunction, however, can lead to serious diseases such as heart attacks and strokes. To determine the likelihood of dysfunction, researchers and clinicians can utilise tools called platelet function tests (PFTs), which can provide an array of insights to platelet function. Historically, these have been performed in labs with a relatively high equipment and specialism burden. More recently, a new type of platelet function test that can be performed in a wider range of settings was introduced – the point of care test (POCT). However, the sensitivity, specificity, and cost of these tests has significantly limited their utility. Here, we show a novel, proof-of-concept POCT which has the required characteristics to mee

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint/provisional research contribution (dated 2026-04-25, categorized cs.AI but describing experimental medical device development). The abstract is incomplete ('mee' truncated), indicating preliminary status. The work presents proof-of-concept validation rather than established clinical fact. Claims about sensitivity/specificity improvements are not yet peer-reviewed or validated in clinical practice.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 微小な流れが特異性を通して増幅され、コスト逆数の立方根で医療へのアクセスが解放される。
